morta_nuts2
  • Mortality Projection Engine
  • View page source

Mortality Projection Engine

Projection

This module provides classes and utilities for prospective mortality projection, high-age extrapolation, life expectancy computation, and actuarial annuity pricing.

Main components

  • kannisto_log_mu() : Low-level Kannisto log-mortality formula.

  • LifeExpectancy : Computes period life expectancy from mortality rates.

  • ProjectorLC : Lee-Carter projection.

  • ProjectorLL : Li-Lee two-factor projection (common + regional).

  • HighAgeExtrapolator : Extrapolates log-mortality beyond the observed age range.

  • concat_logmu_time() : Concatenates historical and projected log-mortality arrays.

  • Annuity_pricing() : Actuarial present value of life annuities.

  • compute_mae() : Mean absolute error and weighted MAE between observed and modelled rates.

morta_nuts2.model.projection.project_class.kannisto_log_mu(x, a, b)[source]

Evaluate the log force of mortality under the Kannisto model.

The Kannisto (logistic Gompertz) parametric model expresses the force of mortality as a logistic function of age:

\[\mu(x) = \frac{a \, e^{bx}}{1 + a \, e^{bx}}\]

This function returns \(\log \mu(x)\) directly for numerical stability (avoids computing exp then log).

Parameters:

  • x (array-like) – Ages at which to evaluate the model.

  • a (float) – Scale parameter (\(a > 0\)). Controls the level of mortality.

  • b (float) – Growth parameter (\(b > 0\)). Controls the rate of mortality increase with age.

Returns:

\(\log \mu(x)\) evaluated at each age in x.

Return type:

ndarray

Notes

As \(x \to \infty\), \(\mu(x) \to 1\), so the model imposes an implicit upper bound of 1 on the force of mortality — a key difference from the unbounded Gompertz model.

class morta_nuts2.model.projection.project_class.LifeExpectancy(mu_future: ndarray)[source]

Bases: object

Computes period life expectancy from an array of force-of-mortality rates.

Period life expectancy at age \(x\) is defined as the expected number of additional years lived by a cohort subject throughout its life to the age-specific mortality rates observed at a given point in time:

\[e_x = \frac{\sum_{y=x}^{\omega} {}_y p_0}{{}_{x} p_0} = \sum_{t=0}^{\omega - x} {}_t p_x\]

where the survival probability is derived from the force of mortality via:

\[{}_t p_x = \exp\!\left(-\int_x^{x+t} \mu(s)\, ds\right) \approx \prod_{k=0}^{t-1} \exp(-\mu_{x+k})\]
Parameters:

mu_future (ndarray) –

Array of force-of-mortality rates.

  • Shape (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions) for the deterministic case.

  • Shape (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions, n_sim) for the stochastic case.

compute() → ndarray[source]

Compute life expectancy for the full mu_future array.

Returns:

Life expectancy array with the same shape as mu_future:

  • (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions) in the deterministic case.

  • (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions, n_sim) in the stochastic case.

Return type:

ndarray

class morta_nuts2.model.projection.project_class.ProjectorLC(results: dict, tv, horizon: int = 30, exclude_years: list = None, nb_components: int = 1, model: str = 'rw', stochastic: bool = True, n_sim: int = 1000)[source]

Bases: object

The Lee-Carter model decomposes the log force of mortality as:

\[\ln \mu(x, t) = \alpha_x + \beta_x \, \kappa_t + \varepsilon_{x,t}\]

where \(\alpha_x\) is the age-specific baseline, \(\beta_x\) is the age-sensitivity pattern, and \(\kappa_t\) is the period index.

For the multi-regional variant, the period indices across regions are decomposed via SVD:

\[K = U \, S \, V^\top \approx Z_{\text{red}} \, V_{\text{red}}^\top\]

where \(Z_{\text{red}} = U_{[1:r]} S_{[1:r]}\) collects the first nb_components principal scores and \(V_{\text{red}}\) the corresponding regional loadings.

Two period-index dynamics are supported:

Random walk with drift (``model=’rw’``):

\[Z_{T+h} = Z_T + \sum_{s=1}^{h} \varepsilon_s, \qquad \varepsilon_s \sim \mathcal{N}(\hat{\delta},\, \hat{\Sigma})\]

Linear trend (``model=’linear’``):

\[Z_t = \alpha + \beta \, t + \varepsilon_t, \qquad \varepsilon_t \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Sigma_{\text{res}})\]
Parameters:

  • results (dict) – Must contain results["curves"]["alpha_x"], results["curves"]["beta_xg"], and results["parameters"]["kappa"].

  • tv (array-like) – Observation year vector.

  • horizon (int, optional) – Number of projection years (default 30).

  • exclude_years (list, optional) – Years to exclude before fitting (default [2020, 2021]).

  • nb_components (int, optional) – Number of SVD components to retain (default 1).

  • model ({'rw', 'linear'}, optional) – Projection model for \(\kappa_t\) (default 'rw').

  • stochastic (bool, optional) – If True, generates n_sim Monte-Carlo paths (default True).

  • n_sim (int, optional) – Number of stochastic simulations (default 1000).

project() → dict[source]

Run the full Lee-Carter projection and return all outputs.

Dispatches to the appropriate internal methods depending on whether kappa is scalar (classic 1-D variant) or multi-regional (SVD variant), then calls _reconstruct() to apply the Lee-Carter identity and produce mortality arrays.

Returns:

Deterministic keys: kappa_future, logmu_future, mu_future.

Stochastic keys: kappa_paths, kappa_lower, kappa_median, kappa_upper, logmu_sim, mu_sim, mu_lower, mu_median, mu_upper.

Return type:

dict

class morta_nuts2.model.projection.project_class.ProjectorLL(results: dict, tv, horizon: int = 30, exclude_years: list = None, nb_components: int = 1, model: str = 'rw', stochastic: bool = True, n_sim: int = 1000)[source]

Bases: object

Li-Lee prospective mortality projection (common factor + regional components).

The Li-Lee model extends the Lee-Carter model by decomposing log-mortality into a common trend shared by all regions and region-specific deviations:

\[\ln \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} = \alpha_x^{(g)} + \beta_x \, \kappa_t + \beta_x^{(g)} \, \kappa_t^{(g)}\]

where:

  • \(\alpha_x^{(g)}\) is the age-region baseline log-mortality.

  • \(\beta_x\) and \(\kappa_t\) are the common age-loading and time-index (shared across regions).

  • \(\beta_x^{(g)}\) and \(\kappa_t^{(g)}\) are the regional age-loading and time-index (region \(g\)-specific).

The regional kappa series \(\kappa_t^{(g)}\) is compressed via SVD into nb_components principal components before projection.

Parameters:

  • results (dict) –

    Estimation output. Must contain:

    • results["curves"] (parametric variant) with keys alpha_xg, beta_x, beta_xg, or

    • results["parameters"] (classic variant) with keys ax, bx, bx_gr, kappa, kappa_gr.

  • tv (array-like) – Observation year vector aligned with kappa.

  • horizon (int, optional) – Number of projection years (default 30).

  • exclude_years (list, optional) – Years excluded from drift/covariance estimation (default [2020, 2021]).

  • nb_components (int, optional) – Number of SVD components retained for the regional kappa (default 1).

  • model ({'rw', 'linear'}, optional) – Time-series model for kappa projection (default 'rw').

  • stochastic (bool, optional) – If True, generate Monte Carlo paths (default True).

  • n_sim (int, optional) – Number of stochastic simulations (default 1000).

project() → dict[source]

Run the full Lee-Li projection and return all outputs.

Masks excluded years, compresses regional kappas via SVD, projects both the common and regional factors with the selected time-series model, then calls _reconstruct() to apply the Lee-Li identity and build mortality arrays.

Returns:

Deterministic keys: kappa_future, kappa_g_future, logmu_future, mu_future.

Stochastic keys: kappa_paths, kappa_lower, kappa_median, kappa_upper, kappa_g_paths, kappa_g_lower, kappa_g_median, kappa_g_upper, logmu_sim, mu_sim, mu_lower, mu_median, mu_upper.

Return type:

dict

class morta_nuts2.model.projection.project_class.HighAgeExtrapolator(xv, x_extrap: int, x_extrap_start, log_Muxtg: ndarray, method: str = 'kannisto', auto_start: bool = False, fallback_linear: bool = True)[source]

Bases: object

Extrapolates log-mortality rates beyond the maximum observed age up to x_extrap.

Two extrapolation methods are available:

Linear (no-intercept regression)

A slope \(s\) is estimated by anchored no-intercept OLS on ages \([x_{\text{start}}, x_{\max}]\):

\[\log\mu(x) - \log\mu(x_{\max}) \approx s \cdot (x - x_{\max})\]

and the extrapolation beyond \(x_{\max}\) is:

\[\log\mu(x) = \log\mu(x_{\max}) + s \cdot (x - x_{\max}), \quad x > x_{\max}\]

Kannisto (logistic Gompertz)

The force of mortality is modelled as:

\[\mu(x) = \frac{a \, e^{bx}}{1 + a \, e^{bx}}\]

Parameters \((a, b)\) are fitted by nonlinear least squares on ages \([x_{\text{start}}, x_{\max}]\) using the kannisto_log_mu() function. If the fit fails, the method falls back to the linear approach. Monotonicity of log-mortality is enforced beyond \(x_{\max}\).

Parameters:

  • xv (array-like) – Observed age vector.

  • x_extrap (int) – Maximum age to extrapolate to.

  • x_extrap_start (int or None) – Starting age of the regression window. Set to None and pass auto_start=True to select automatically via leave-one-out CV.

  • log_Muxtg (ndarray) –

    Log force-of-mortality array. Expected shapes:

    • (nb_ages, nb_regions, horizon) — deterministic, linear method.

    • (nb_ages, nb_regions, horizon, n_sim) — stochastic, linear method.

    • (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions) — deterministic, Kannisto method.

    • (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions, n_sim) — stochastic, Kannisto method.

  • method ({'kannisto', 'linear'}, optional) – Extrapolation method (default 'kannisto').

  • auto_start (bool, optional) – If True, select x_extrap_start automatically (default False).

  • fallback_linear (bool, optional) – If True, fall back to the linear method when Kannisto fails (default True).

extrapolate() → tuple[source]

Run the high-age extrapolation using the selected method.

If auto_start=True or x_extrap_start is None, the optimal regression start age is selected first via leave-one-out cross-validation (_optimal_start()).

Then dispatches to:

  • _extrapolate_linear() for method='linear'.

  • _extrapolate_kannisto() for method='kannisto'.

Returns:

  • out (ndarray) – Extended log-mortality array covering ages up to x_extrap.

  • xv_full (ndarray) – Complete age vector from xv.min() to x_extrap.

Raises:

ValueError – If method is not 'linear' or 'kannisto'.

morta_nuts2.model.projection.project_class.concat_logmu_time(logmu_hist, logmu_proj)[source]

Concatenate historical and projected log-mortality arrays along the time axis.

In the stochastic case, the 3-D historical array is broadcast to 4-D by repeating it n_sim times along a new simulation axis before concatenation, so that the output always has a consistent shape:

\[\log\boldsymbol{\mu}_{\text{full}} = \bigl[\,\log\boldsymbol{\mu}_{\text{hist}} \;\big|\; \log\boldsymbol{\mu}_{\text{proj}}\,\bigr]_{\text{axis=1}}\]
Parameters:

  • logmu_hist (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years_hist, nb_regions)) – Observed log-mortality array.

  • logmu_proj (ndarray) –

    Projected log-mortality array.

    • Deterministic: shape (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions).

    • Stochastic: shape (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions, n_sim).

Returns:

  • Deterministic: shape (nb_ages, nb_years_hist + horizon, nb_regions).

  • Stochastic: shape (nb_ages, nb_years_hist + horizon, nb_regions, n_sim).

Return type:

ndarray

Raises:

ValueError – If logmu_proj is not 3-D or 4-D.

morta_nuts2.model.projection.project_class.Annuity_pricing(xe, xv, log_Muxtg, duration, rate)[source]

Compute the present value of life annuities using simulated mortality rates.

This function evaluates the actuarial present value of a life annuity for different entry ages, regions, and stochastic mortality scenarios. The mortality intensity is provided in logarithmic form and transformed to obtain the force of mortality.

The annuity is valued by computing survival probabilities year by year and discounting future payments using a constant interest rate.

Parameters:

  • xe (array-like) – Vector of entry ages at which the annuity starts.

  • xv (array-like) – Vector of ages in the mortality table (not directly used in the function but typically included for compatibility with the mortality grid).

  • log_Muxtg (numpy.ndarray) –

    4D array containing the logarithm of mortality intensities with shape: (age, projection_year, region, simulation).

    Dimensions: - age: index corresponding to the age in the mortality table - projection_year: future year index - region: geographical region - simulation: stochastic mortality scenario

  • duration (int) – Maximum duration (in years) of the annuity payments.

  • rate (float) – Constant annual interest rate used for discounting.

Returns:

Array containing the annuity prices with shape: (len(xe), number_of_regions, number_of_simulations)

Each value corresponds to the actuarial present value of a life annuity for a given entry age, region, and stochastic mortality scenario.

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

Notes

The survival probability is approximated using the force of mortality:

\[p_x = \exp(-\mu_x)\]

where \(\mu_x\) is the force of mortality.

The annuity value is computed as:

\[a_x = \sum_{t=1}^{T} v^t \cdot {}_t p_x\]

where:

  • \(v = \frac{1}{1+i}\) is the discount factor

  • \({}_t p_x\) is the survival probability from age \(x\) to \(x+t\)

  • \(T\) is the annuity duration

The function evaluates the annuity value for each: - entry age - region - stochastic mortality simulation.

morta_nuts2.model.projection.project_class.compute_mae(mu_obs, mu_model, weights=None)[source]

Compute the Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Weighted MAE (WMAE) between observed and modelled mortality rates.

The global MAE is:

\[\text{MAE} = \frac{1}{A \cdot T \cdot G} \sum_{x,t,g} \bigl| \hat{\mu}_{x,t}^{(g)} - \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \bigr|\]

When exposure weights \(E_{x,t}^{(g)}\) are provided, the weighted MAE is:

\[\text{WMAE} = \frac{ \sum_{x,t,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \bigl| \hat{\mu}_{x,t}^{(g)} - \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \bigr| }{ \sum_{x,t,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} }\]

Both metrics are also computed marginally over each dimension (by region, by age, by year).

Parameters:

  • mu_obs (ndarray, shape (nb_ages, horizon) or (nb_ages, horizon, nb_regions)) – Observed mortality rates.

  • mu_model (ndarray) – Modelled mortality rates; must be broadcastable to the shape of mu_obs.

  • weights (ndarray or None, optional) – Non-negative weights (e.g. exposures \(E_{x,t}^{(g)}\)), same shape as mu_obs. If None, only unweighted MAE is computed.

Returns:

Always contains:

  • "by_region" — MAE averaged over ages and years, shape (nb_regions,).

  • "by_age" — MAE averaged over years and regions, shape (nb_ages,).

  • "by_year" — MAE averaged over ages and regions, shape (horizon,).

  • "global" — scalar global MAE.

If weights is provided, also contains:

  • "wmae_by_region", "wmae_by_age", "wmae_by_year", "wmae_global".

Return type:

dict
